person.name: Seokho Son
person.bio: Seokho Son is a senior researcher and open source special fellow at the Electronics and Telecommunications Research Institute (ETRI), a national research institute in South Korea. His primary responsibilities include designing systems and devising algorithms for cloud and cloud-native computing to accomplish national research projects. He is one of the maintainers of the CNCF Cloud Native Glossary which aims to define cloud native concepts in clear and simple language, and he is contributing to Kubernetes by leading the Korean localization team in Kubernetes SIG-Docs and maintaining localization in Kubernetes/website. Furthermore, he is one of the maintainers of Cloud-Barista, a multi-cloud management platform. As an open source advocate, Seokho has served as a speaker at various cloud native events, including Cloud Native Community Groups and Kubernetes Forum in South Korea, and he is actively involved in mentoring open source contribution programs in South Korea.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am thrilled to have become a CNCF Ambassador and excited to continue contributing to cloud-native technology. I have been working on reducing language barriers in cloud-native technologies by localizing technical documents in Kubernetes, and maintaining the CNCF Cloud Native Glossary project to make cloud-native concepts accessible to anyone, regardless of their technical background. Through my community activities, I have had the pleasure of meeting many talented and friendly contributors, and I look forward to continue developing these relationships. I believe that being a CNCF ambassador is an extraordinary opportunity for me to spread cloud-native technologies even further. As a CNCF Ambassador, I am aiming to be a reliable source of information for the Korean community by sharing the latest technology information and community trends in a timely manner, and I hope to inspire Korean experts to get involved in global activities.

person.bio: I earned my PhD in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Victoria, Victoria, BC, Canada, where my research was focused on computer networks and wireless communications. Building on this academic foundation, I now work as a senior Site Reliability Engineer at Fortinet, specializing in cloud-native technologies, Kubernetes, containerization, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), and infrastructure automation. My work bridges deep research expertise with practical engineering to ensure the reliability and scalability of cloud systems.

person.name: Sagar Utekar
person.bio: Working as MTS3 Site Reliability Engineer at VMWare Software India PVT. LTD. Cloud Native Day Pune (CND Pune’23) Organizer Kubernetes Community Day Pune (KCD’24) Organizer Open-Source Enthusiast, A firm believer in the power of community education. Google Summer Of Code Admin since 2018, Mentor, Mentee 2022 @PEcAn Project GSoD, GSSoC, Github Externship, SIH mentor CKS | CKA | CKAD | Terraform Certified Mentored 10k+ students & professionals for free across India for career guidance, Job Search, Open Source, Cloud Native Docker & CNCG Pune community leader Enjoys photography, Acting, Teaching, Trekking, fitnessWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I have been organising monthly meetups under CNCG Pune chapter, have been sharing my knowledge by speaking at different events, conferences, rural area colleges, schools, local meet-up groups to spread awareness and share knowledge about cloud native. By becoming CNCF Ambassador I will get more support, training, mentorship, guidance and rewards to build stronger community which will open wide door of the opportunities - to network with other community leaders, CNCF leaders and share my knowledge to wider audience. As part of CNCF Ambassador, it will give me opportunity to network with equally passionate Ambassadors from all over the globe. CNCF Ambassador gets support, mentorship, trainings, funding to host the events which will help me a lot to foster a strong community and upcoming events. It will give me the opportunity to promote Cloud Native technologies projects globally.

person.name: Vladimir Traikovich
person.bio: I’m a technology enthusiast who enjoys learning and experimenting with modern infrastructure, especially software-defined storage and cloud-native platforms.

person.name: Zaw Ye Aung
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with 11+ years of experience in IT, built on a strong foundation in system and infrastructure engineering. Hands-on with Kubernetes platforms, GitOps-based CI/CD, and scalable cloud and on-prem environments, with a strong focus on reliability, automation, and cloud-native technologies. Actively working toward becoming a CNCF contributor and growing as a platform engineer focused on building resilient, secure systems.

person.name: Daniel Krook
person.bio: Daniel Krook is focused on better serving the maintainers, contributors, and users in the community of 200+ open source projects hosted by the CNCF. He has been involved in the use, creation, and advocacy of open source technology for over 20 years. He partnered with the LF to establish the "Call for Code with The Linux Foundation" umbrella and has guided 16 projects through the process of joining the LF and using LFX tools.He has built software end-to-end for a wide array of industries and earned certifications in cloud architecture, application development, and system operations along the way. Most recently, he worked at IBM as a principal engineer developing first-of-a-kind solutions that integrate open source technologies with commercial products and services.Daniel has spoken at many open source conferences (including main-stage presentations at the first CloudNativeDay and the first KubeCon run by the CNCF), hosted dozens of meetups (primarily OpenWhisk, Cloud Foundry, OpenStack, and PHP), and has contributed to many technical blogs, videos, podcasts, articles, and books.

person.name: Filipe Magalhães
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with a strong focus on cloud-native technologies, container orchestration, and platform engineering. Experienced in managing and operating Kubernetes and OpenShift clusters across both cloud and on-premises environments. Passionate about building scalable, secure, and resilient infrastructure, and contributing to the adoption of CNCF technologies within the organization

person.name: Gnani Rahul Nutakki
person.bio: I am a Principal Cloud Platform Engineer at Radiant Logic with over eight years of experience in cloud automation, Kubernetes, Terraform, and modern infrastructure design. I hold multiple Kubernetes and AWS certifications, focusing on building secure, scalable, and efficient platform systems across multi-cloud environments. My work addresses critical challenges in agentic AI security, discovery, and management. I am also passionate about cloud-native technologies and continuous learning, regularly sharing insights through on my Medium publication Clear-Text.

person.name: Jakub Hovorka
person.bio: I'm a DevOps Engineer from the Netherlands passionate about Kubernetes and GitOps. My favorite tools within the CNCF landscape are ArgoCD, Helm, and Prometheus.

person.name: Leonardo Gonçalves Da Silva
person.bio: I’m a Senior Solutions Architect and Cybersecurity Engineering Manager with extensive experience in cloud-native technologies and microservices architecture. I specialize in Kubernetes and many other CNCF projects, helping organizations design, build, and operate scalable, secure, and resilient cloud infrastructures.

person.name: Loc Mai
person.bio: Like many others here, I'm a tech enthusiast with a strong passion for cloud infrastructure, scalable and distributed systems, and Kubernetes.  When I'm not immersed in tech, I enjoy playing video games.  When I decide to step away from the screen and touch some grass, I travel to explore other cultures (through their food)!

person.name: Sinan Ulutaş
person.bio: I’m a System Architect with a strong passion for open-source technologies. I have extensive experience in Linux, Virtualization, Load Balancing, Kubernetes, Containerization, Automation, and Cloud solutions. I specialize in designing and managing high-load systems, particularly in high-traffic environments. I’m a true open-source enthusiast and always eager to stay up-to-date with the latest technologies.

person.name: Victor Caceres Chian
person.bio: Data Engineer focused on building scalable data platforms, ETL pipelines, and CI/CD workflows in cloud-native environments. Interested in Kubernetes and platform engineering, with a continuous learning mindset.

person.name: Biodun
person.bio: Cloud infrastructure and Platform Engineering interest. I also provide DevOps, SRE, and Security solutions to support next gen workloads on Kubernetes, using CNCF technologies and do more with the open-source community.

person.name: Chad M. Crowell
person.bio: Chad is a Principal SRE at Akamai, and author of the book "Acing the Certified Kubernetes Administrator Exam". Chad has been an instructor for over six years on platforms such as A Cloud Guru (now Pluralsight) and INE. Through KubeSkills, he helps people find fun and interactive ways to learn Kubernetes, Linux, and containers.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I really enjoy helping people get involved in open source software. I have realized the power of community, in lifting others up and succeeding together, and I love to share it with others. I believe it is my duty in life to share what I've learned in order to make intellectual progress as a human race.

person.name: Jakub Hoborowicz
person.bio: Former military and a passionate DevOps Engineer with over 3 years of production experience, driven by a strong interest in DevSecOps. I began my journey through self-learning, primarily programming in Python, until I discovered topics like Kubernetes and cloud computing. Since then, I’ve dedicated my time to deepening my knowledge in these areas and continuously expanding my horizons, while also sharing and exchanging insights with others. Currently working as a DevOps Engineer for the Polish government, where I help drive digital transformation by building and maintaining secure, scalable infrastructure and automating delivery pipelines — all to bring digital services closer to citizens.

person.name: Roushdy Ali
person.bio: Roushdy is a network and cloud senior consulting engineer with extensive experience designing and implementing network, virtualization, and cloud solutions. He has a keen interest in open-source and Cloud-Native technologies, particularly Kubernetes.

person.name: Zaynul Abedin Miah
person.bio: Cloud-native community leader, Co-Organizer Azure Tech Group Bangladesh and  AWS Cloud Club Captain at BRAC University, passionate about empowering students through open source and cloud technologies. As Bangladesh's first AWS Cloud Club to reach 1,238+ members, Zaynul has hosted global events featuring AWS leadership, trained 15+ tech professionals in cloud certifications in his study group, and organized KCD Chattogram 2024 with 2,000+ participants. Active contributor on dev.to AWS and Microsoft tag moderation, AWS Builder Center technical blogs, and recognized in AWS PartyRock Hackathon. Driven by democratizing cloud-native education across South Asia, he bridges students with global ecosystems while promoting inclusive tech communities.

person.name: Massinissa Kherfallah
person.bio: I am a Tech Lead DevOps at Société Générale with a strong expertise in the Public Cloud Azure and FinOps.

person.name: Michael Hofer
person.bio: I'm passionate about open source, especially being part of OpenBao's Technical Steering Committee (TSC) and its community to drive sustainable secrets management! Let's collaborate!

person.name: Ihor Dvoretskyi
person.bio:
Ihor is a Senior Developer Advocate at Cloud Native Computing Foundation, focused on Kubernetes-related efforts in the open source community.

He is a Product Manager for Kubernetes, co-leading the Product Management Special Interest Group, focused on enhancing Kubernetes as an open source product. Besides that, he is participating in Kubernetes release process as a features lead for multiple Kubernetes releases.

A Linux user since 2005 with a deep engineering background, Ihor has been responsible for projects tightly bound to the cloud computing space, containerized workloads and Linux systems.

His recent speaking appearances include talks at Kubernetes Leadership and Contributors Summits, OpenStack Summits NA and APAC, OpenSource Summit EU, ContainerDays EU, Google DevFests and other industry-leading events.

person.name: Kylash Mukund Rameshbapu
person.bio: I am currently working as a DevOps Engineer with over 4 years of combined experience in managing ETL workloads and deploying cloud applications using DevOps practices. Recently, I transitioned into the DevOps domain, where I have built hands-on expertise working with various Devops tools. Having the Kubestronaut credential demonstrates my commitment to continuous learning and my ability to adapt to new technologies. I am excited to leverage my skills and contribute to the Cloud Native community in the coming years.

person.name: Sudha Raghavan
person.bio:
Sudha is the VP for all the services in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Developer Platform. OCI’s developer services aim to improve productivity for all roles performed by Developer Security Operations engineers. Enabling a secure, fast, and connected developer platform across all Oracle internal and external cloud engineers is her team’s goal. She prides herself in running numerous large cloud scale distributed services developed in house using open-source technologies.

She is also a founding committee member of the Oracle Women’s Leadership (OWL), Seattle chapter and runs several mentoring rings. She has a Masters in Computer Science and has previously worked at Microsoft Bing. She is a goal-driven technology leader with a passion to attract more women into leadership positions.

person.name: Aaron Zhilong Wang
person.bio: Zhilong Wang is a Principal Engineer specializing in cloud-native and distributed systems, with expertise in Serverless, Service Mesh, and cloud platforms. He has 10+ years of core architecture and practical engineering experience at Tencent, Alibaba, and JD.com, continuously improving the performance and reliability of cloud-native systems under data-intensive and compute-intensive workloads, including complex environments involving GPU/CPU heterogeneous resource coordination and mixed online–offline deployments. He also actively contributes to CNCF open-source projects such as Kubernetes, MOSN, and Layotto.

person.name: Daisuke Watarai
person.bio: I technically lead application and cloud development as a Cloud Engineer. My expertise covers front-end, CI/CD, containers, and network/security, including practical experience with Kubernetes. I am passionate about AWS and cloud-native tech, and am committed to continuous learning to advance my skills.

person.name: Michał D.
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with Kubernetes expertise since 2019. Mastered multi-cloud environments through hands-on challenges. Committed to creating simple solutions for complex problems.

person.name: Ravi G. Palankar
person.bio: Cloud/DevOps Engineer with extensive experience in containerization, AWS cloud infrastructure, and automation. Specializes in implementing and managing Kubernetes clusters, CI/CD pipelines, and monitoring solutions. Strong focus on security implementation and infrastructure optimization. Passionate about building scalable solutions and optimizing development workflows.

person.name: Subhash Polisetti
person.bio: While most people dream of electric sheep, I ponder about the tech~no~logical singularity while orchestrating containers [aka tesseracts], debugging AI infrastructure whilst not summoning any basilisks to bootload humanity up the Kardashev scale. I occasionally transmit thoughts about building things at log.101010.dev (whenever I escape the event horizon of my todo list). For more mysterious adven-char[c]s from this localhost, resolve my hostname at port 42 (ARPANET~style) on 127001.page ⏳

person.name: Vivian Hu
person.bio: Vivian Hu is a Product Manager at Second State and a columnist at InfoQ. She is a founding member of the WasmEdge project. She organizes Rust and WebAssembly community events, especially RustChinaConf in Asia.

person.name: Ation Gilliam
person.bio: My name is A’tion Jen’te Gilliam, and I’m an ambitious, goal-oriented individual. When I was younger, I couldn’t have imagined the level of success I would go on to achieve in my career. I’ve overcome the adversity and challenges of growing up in Los Angeles, and those experiences have shaped who I am today. Now, I’m committed to using that journey to teach, motivate, and inspire young people. Through my non-profit organization, The Gift INC, I want to show them that they too can build successful careers in IT.

person.name: Pepijn Oomen
person.bio: With 40+ years experience in the ICT field, Pepijn has been recognised by IBM as an OS/2 Warp Engineer and a Lotus Notes Principal Engineer, by RedHat as a Certified Engineer (RHCE) and a Certified Specialist in OpenShift and more recently by the Linux Foundation as a Kubestronaut.  He is a long time user of containers and Kubernetes and has previously been providing on-site trainings for RedHat OpenShift.  As a Platform Engineer, Pepijn works with a focus on security, reliability and automation for complex infrastructures.

person.name: Rob Kenefeck
person.bio: I've been Automating toil in Linux for over 20 years, but when Kubernetes came along I was initially dismissive, after all I was already building everything in Containers with Docker and orchestrating in a variety of semi-manual ways. I've since come to appreciate all Kubernetes can do and also what its limitations are and when NOT to use it. I've taught DevOps courses and Kubernetes courses while also helping large enterprises setup internally managed Kubernetes platforms with a product based mindset. I believe that security is so fundamental to everything that we do, we shouldn't be shoving it in as an afterthought with DevSecOps. Now, you'll find me at Cloud Native Melbourne (or almost any other Kubernetes/Platform Engineering meetup in Melbourne), organising CloudCon AU or spruiking Toastmasters. I'll be virtually present in FINOS Common Cloud controls or the APAC Security and Cpmpliance TAG

person.name: Ashish Shah
person.bio: I am passionate about programming and automation. Technical Capabilities include: Kubernetes, Amazon Web Services, Shell Scripting, Python,Java and SQL Love solving problems.

person.name: Vincent De Nardo
person.bio: I’m a DevSecOps engineer and cloud enthusiast with a strong focus on Microsoft Azure and cloud-native architectures. I enjoy working at the intersection of development, security and operations, building secure and reliable infrastructure through automation. I’m particularly passionate about Kubernetes and open-source projects, actively exploring tools and practices from the CNCF ecosystem. I’m eager to continuously learn, share my knowledge with the community, and collaborate in teams to design practical, secure and scalable solutions.

person.name: Xiaohui Zhang
person.bio: Senior cloud native architect and evangelist at Flomesh. I am a seasoned programmer, LF APAC open source evangelist, CNCF Ambassador, member of the cloud native community committee(China local) and Microsoft Azure MVP. With many years of microservice and infrastructure practice experience, mainly involving microservices, containers, Kubernetes, DevOps, etc. In the past there years, I organize two meetups in Guangzhou and Service Mesh Conference in Shanghai together with community members. In the coming April 15, I will organize KCD in Dalian with other community members. I own a Chinese blog https://atbug.com and start writing on Medium https://addozhang.medium.com.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Being a CNCF ambassador is a recognition of my involvement in open source and cloud-native technology promotion over the past few years. After becoming a CNCF ambassador, I can better promote CNCF technology and organize more in-person and virtual events. At the same time, I can also receive more support from CNCF.

person.name: Jakub Granieczny
person.bio: I’m an engineer passionate about building scalable, cloud-native platforms and helping teams get the most out of modern infrastructure. My journey has taken me from supporting large scientific systems at CERN to shaping fast-moving environments at an AI startup in Zurich. I enjoy working with Kubernetes and GitOps, contributing to the open-source ecosystem, and staying deeply engaged with the cloud-native community.
